title: Missing MULTIDRIVEN warning for variable with multiple drivers
body:
Compilation of such module
```SystemVerilog
module a(output bit b);
  or g(b, c, d);
  assign b = 'b1;
endmodule: a
```
prints only IMPLICIT warnings.


### Expected behavior
In the example `b` has 2 drivers: the output of gate and the continuous assignment. However, `b` is declared as a variable type, which means it should not have multiple drivers.

According to the IEEE specification:
> Variables can only be driven by one continuous assignment or by one primitive output or module output.

In this situation, an error or at least a MULTIDRIVEN warning should be printed, indicating that a singledriven connection has multiple drivers

Options: `--cc --exe testbench.cpp --timing -Wno-fatal +1800-2023ext+sv`

This issue occurs in `Verilator 5.030 2024-10-27 rev v5.030-45-g2cb1a8de7`

Found by a [verilog model](https://github.com/DepTyCheck/verilog-model) using [deptycheck](https://github.com/buzden/deptycheck)
